rationale: Review 1's single finding (make `antipode_convMul_id` private) is real and grounded in the diff; acting on it improves the API without risk. Review 2's three findings include one fabricated claim (no `antipodeAlgHom` is promised anywhere in the diff) and one likely-wrong claim (dropping `noncomputable` from definitions that depend on the noncomputable antipode would break compilation), so acting on Review 2 would produce a worse PR.
